steps.rationale: The strongest combined bet is to improve both factual retention and final note quality: preserve more source detail upstream and use a more capable note generator with a structured clinical style downstream.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact fails as a clinical note. It captures only the chief complaint at a very high level, omits nearly all required clinical content, provides no assessment or plan, and includes obvious non-clinical leakage about pipeline settings and demo profiles. Overall, it is far from the expected note in both substance and presentation.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Higher-fidelity pipeline for chart-ready follow-up notes with better retention and stronger generation.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Higher-fidelity pipeline for chart-ready follow-up notes with better retention and stronger generation.`
params -> pipeline config -> asr -> model changed from `parakeet` to `parakeet`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> model changed from `gpt-5-mini` to `gpt-5`
params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> prompt style changed from `brief` to `clinical_structured`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> denoise changed from `off` to `on`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> silence trimming changed from `aggressive` to `conservative`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> vad changed from `strict` to `moderate`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> voice enhancement changed from `off` to `on`

steps.rationale: The note is missing concrete facts that likely dropped earlier in the pipeline. Relaxing overly aggressive preprocessing should preserve more timeline, meds, allergy, negatives, and return-precaution details before note generation.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact fails as a clinical follow-up note. It preserves only a small fraction of the case facts, omits all actionable clinical content, and includes non-clinical leakage that does not belong in the note. Despite being readable as a sentence, it is neither complete nor professionally presented, so the overall performance is very poor.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Recall-oriented preprocessing with less aggressive trimming and VAD plus light enhancement.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Recall-oriented preprocessing with less aggressive trimming and VAD plus light enhancement.`

params -> pipeline config -> note generation -> prompt style changed from `brief` to `brief`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> denoise changed from `off` to `on`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> silence trimming changed from `aggressive` to `conservative`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> vad changed from `strict` to `moderate`
params -> pipeline config -> preprocessing -> voice enhancement changed from `off` to `on`

steps.rationale: The main failure is note-generation behavior: the output leaks pipeline/demo commentary, lacks SOAP-style structure, and omits plan/precautions. Tightening the note prompt toward a chart-ready clinical format is the lowest-risk first move without changing the overall pipeline.
steps.judge_justification: The artifact fails as a clinical evaluation note. It captures almost none of the clinically important content, provides no actionable structure or plan, and introduces non-clinical meta language that does not belong in the chart. Despite passing the contract, the note quality against the rubric is very poor and warrants near-minimal scores across dimensions.

changes.before: Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.
changes.after: Structured clinical note prompt that forbids pipeline/meta commentary and requires assessment, plan, and return precautions.
changes.summary: description changed from `Weak baseline deterministic demo with rough language and vague planning.` to `Structured clinical note prompt that forbids pipeline/meta commentary and requires assessment, plan, and return precautions.`
